GitHub Actions — Deploy Production (main branch)
=================================================

Workflow file: .github/workflows/deploy-production.yml

Runs on: every push to `main` (and manual "Run workflow" in Actions tab).

What it does:
  1. Builds `frontend/` and deploys to Netlify
  Backend: Railway project soothing-creativity auto-deploys `backend/` from GitHub (already connected).

----------------------------------------------------------------------
ONE-TIME SETUP — GitHub secrets
----------------------------------------------------------------------

Repo → Settings → Secrets and variables → Actions → New repository secret

1) RAILWAY_TOKEN
   Railway project: soothing-creativity → Project Settings → Tokens
   → Create project token (copy the full token)

2) RAILWAY_SERVICE_ID
   soothing-creativity → production → service ai-interview-copilot → Settings
   Copy the Service ID (UUID). Root directory should be: backend
   Or: cd backend && railway link (pick soothing-creativity) && railway status

3) NETLIFY_AUTH_TOKEN
   Netlify → User settings → Applications → Personal access tokens
   Create token with deploy access

4) NETLIFY_SITE_ID
   Netlify → your site → Site configuration → Site information
   Copy "Site ID" (UUID)

----------------------------------------------------------------------
AVOID DOUBLE DEPLOYS (recommended)
----------------------------------------------------------------------

If Netlify is already connected to GitHub with "Build on push":
  Netlify → Site configuration → Build & deploy → Continuous deployment
  → Stop builds (or disconnect) and let GitHub Actions deploy only.

Railway (soothing-creativity): keep GitHub auto-deploy on main — workflow does NOT redeploy Railway.

Netlify: either Actions (this workflow) OR Netlify GitHub builds — not both.

Pick ONE path for Netlify (native OR Actions), not both.

----------------------------------------------------------------------
MANUAL RUN
----------------------------------------------------------------------

GitHub → Actions → Deploy Production → Run workflow

----------------------------------------------------------------------
RAILWAY ENV VARS (still required on Railway dashboard)
----------------------------------------------------------------------

GEMINI_API_KEY, MONGODB_URI, JWT_SECRET, CORS_ORIGINS, RESEND_API_KEY, etc.
GitHub Actions only uploads code — secrets stay in Railway / Netlify UI.

CORS_ORIGINS example:
  https://YOUR-SITE.netlify.app

----------------------------------------------------------------------
NETLIFY ENV (optional)
----------------------------------------------------------------------

VITE_API_URL is NOT required if using same-origin proxy (netlify.toml + _redirects).
You can remove VITE_API_URL from Netlify if deploy uses latest main code.
